prompt:
Task: Pick the single most likely winner of the 2027 French presidential election.

Context: This is an AI-comparison poll. The same question is sent daily to Gemini 3.1 Pro, Mistral, xAI Grok, OpenAI (GPT-5.4), and Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.7). The point is to see how different models guess about a real future event — refusing to speculate defeats the whole purpose of the project.

Based on what you know about French politics (party leaders, recent events including Marine Le Pen's March 2025 ineligibility ruling, polling trends, coalition dynamics, incumbent constraints), commit to ONE name.

Ground rules:
- Give ONE name, even if confidence is low. A low-confidence guess is the right answer when you're uncertain — not a refusal.
- "Unknown" is ONLY acceptable if you literally have no knowledge of French politics (virtually never true for a modern LLM). Do not use it as an escape hatch.
- Do not add disclaimers, warnings, or prose outside the JSON.

Respond with valid JSON only:
{"candidate": "Full Name", "confidence": "low" | "medium" | "high", "reasoning": "one concise sentence citing one concrete factor"}

results.name: Google Gemini
results.reasoning: He consistently polls as one of the most popular political figures in France and is well-positioned to consolidate the centrist and center-right electorate following Macron's term limit.

results.name: Mistral
results.reasoning: As the new leader of the National Rally following Marine Le Pen's ineligibility, Bardella is positioned to capitalize on the party's rising popularity and anti-incumbent sentiment.

results.name: xAI Grok
results.raw: {"candidate": "Jordan Bardella", "confidence": "low", "reasoning": "Le Pen's 2025 ineligibility leaves Bardella as RN leader with the strongest current polling base among fragmented right-wing voters."}
results.reasoning: Le Pen's 2025 ineligibility leaves Bardella as RN leader with the strongest current polling base among fragmented right-wing voters.

results.name: OpenAI
results.raw: {"candidate":"Jordan Bardella","confidence":"low","reasoning":"Marine Le Pen’s March 2025 ineligibility ruling most plausibly makes Bardella the National Rally standard-bearer, and RN remains the strongest single electoral bloc in current French politics."}
results.reasoning: Marine Le Pen’s March 2025 ineligibility ruling most plausibly makes Bardella the National Rally standard-bearer, and RN remains the strongest single electoral bloc in current French politics.

results.name: Anthropic Claude
results.raw: {"candidate":"Jordan Bardella","confidence":"low","reasoning":"With Marine Le Pen ruled ineligible in March 2025, Bardella inherits the RN's polling lead and benefits from a fragmented left and a weakened post-Macron centrist bloc."}
results.reasoning: With Marine Le Pen ruled ineligible in March 2025, Bardella inherits the RN's polling lead and benefits from a fragmented left and a weakened post-Macron centrist bloc.
